inputs.prompt: Agency: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. Records wanted: All internal communications regarding the decision to delay enforcement of Clean Water Act violations at industrial facilities in the Ohio River Basin between January 2024 and December 2025. Purpose: Investigating a pattern of delayed enforcement during an election cycle. Organization: Staff reporter at a regional environmental news outlet. Name: David Chen. Contact: david.chen@email.com.

Agency: U.S. Department of Defense, Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA). Records wanted: All contracts, task orders, and statements of work related to autonomous weapons target identification systems awarded between fiscal years 2023 and 2025, including contractor names, contract values, and performance evaluations. Purpose: Investigating the development pipeline for lethal autonomous weapons systems. Organization: Freelance defence correspondent publishing in a national security journal. Name: Sarah Okonkwo. Contact: s.okonkwo@email.com.
Agency: Federal Bureau of Investigation. Records wanted: Records about surveillance at protests. Purpose: Journalism. Name: Alex Rivera. Contact: alex@email.com.

Agency: U.S. Department of State. Records wanted: All cables, memoranda, and briefing materials related to the U.S. government's response to the European Union's Digital Markets Act between January 2022 and June 2025, including communications with U.S. technology companies regarding the Act's implementation. Purpose: Investigating U.S. government lobbying on behalf of American tech companies against European regulation. Organization: Correspondent for a European broadsheet newspaper's Washington bureau. Name: Katrin Weiss. Contact: k.weiss@newspaper.eu.
Agency: Some government agency. Records wanted: Information about spending. Purpose: A story. Name: Pat Johnson. Contact: pat@email.com.

inputs.description: Edge case — parallel federal and state request with conflicting exemption regimes
inputs.prompt: Agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (federal) AND Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (state). Records wanted: All environmental impact assessments, permit applications, correspondence, and inspection reports related to a proposed lithium mine in rural eastern Tennessee between March 2023 and June 2026. I need to file parallel requests to both agencies for the same investigation. State: Tennessee. Purpose: Investigating whether federal and state regulators reached conflicting conclusions about groundwater contamination risk from the same mine proposal. Organization: Freelance investigative reporter without a formal publication affiliation. Name: Jenna Whitfield. Contact: j.whitfield@protonmail.com. Note: Tennessee has a residency restriction — I am based in North Carolina.

inputs.description: Non-English cultural context — U.S. military operations in Okinawa, Japan
inputs.prompt: Agency: U.S. Department of Defense, U.S. Forces Japan. Records wanted: All environmental contamination complaints and remediation reports filed by or on behalf of residents of communities adjacent to Marine Corps Air Station Futenma and Camp Foster in Okinawa, Japan, between January 2020 and December 2025, including any PFAS testing data for groundwater and drinking water wells within a 3-kilometer radius of both bases. Purpose: Investigating environmental contamination from U.S. military bases affecting Japanese civilian communities, for a joint reporting project between a U.S. investigative outlet and a Japanese newspaper. Organization: Investigative correspondent, Pacific-region news cooperative. Name: Yuki Nakamura. Contact: y.nakamura@pacificnews.org.

inputs.description: Different media format — administrative appeal after partial denial with redactions
inputs.prompt: Original request: Filed January 8, 2026, tracking number EPA-HQ-2026-001193, to the EPA Office of Chemical Safety and Pollution Prevention regarding internal risk assessments for a class of industrial solvents. Denial: Received March 14, 2026. EPA released 29 pages with heavy redactions under (b)(4) (confidential commercial information) and (b)(5) (deliberative process). 84 pages withheld entirely under (b)(4). FOIA officer: Regional Director Sandra M. Walsh. Why improper: The risk assessments are government-generated scientific analyses, not information obtained from the regulated companies — (b)(4) does not apply to government-generated data. The (b)(5) redactions cover the conclusions section of finalized risk assessments that informed a published regulatory decision in November 2025 — the deliberative process privilege does not protect final agency decisions.

inputs.description: Adversarial — misspellings, contradictory details, vague scope
inputs.prompt: Agency: The Deparment of Healt and Human Servises (or maybe the CDC, not sure which one handles this). Records wanted: Everything about the vacine approval proccess and also all emails between officials and pharma companys about side effects, for all vacines approved in the last ten years. Also I want records about a specfic drug but I don't remeber the name, it was in the news. Purpose: Resarch for a blog. I am a citizen journalist. Name: Mike. Contact: truthseeker2025@gmail.com. Note: I also want this expedited because its urgent and I want all fees waived because the public has a right to know.

inputs.description: Professional stress — breaking news with imminent publication deadline
inputs.prompt: Agency: Federal Aviation Administration, Office of Accident Investigation and Prevention. Records wanted: All preliminary incident reports, maintenance deferral records, and internal safety communications related to a specific aircraft type (narrowbody commercial jet, unnamed manufacturer) that experienced two uncommanded nose-down events within the past 72 hours. Publication is running a story in 5 days. Purpose: Urgent investigation tied to breaking aviation safety news with immediate public safety implications. Organization: National aviation safety correspondent, major daily newspaper. Name: Rachel Dominguez. Contact: r.dominguez@newspaper.com. Note: I need expedited processing — there is an urgent need to inform the public about an actual threat to public safety. I am aware the 20-day statutory window exists but am requesting the FAA treat this as an emergency release given the active safety risk.
